creator: dgilbert@velocet.ca
text:
>>>>> "Doug" == Doug Barton <DougB@DougBarton.net> writes:

Doug> dgilbert@velocet.ca wrote:
>> Some time ago, I added the GLE port to the tree with the intention
>> that I'd ask for it to be included here.  When GLE is added to
>> XScreenSaver, several cool screen hacks are added to the tree.

Doug> 	I actually evaluated this myself not too long ago. Frankly, I
Doug> wasn't impressed with the few hacks that use the extrusion
Doug> library, and defintely wasn't impressed enough to add the
Doug> dependency. This port already has a lot of them, and I didn't
Doug> think the cost::benefit was high enough. However, now that
Doug> someone has actually ported libgle, it's worth another look I
Doug> suppose.

libgle was an easy port to make and I am the maintainer.  I'd be
willing to maintain xscreensaver, too ... since I usally track it very 
closely anyways... but I don't want to step on anyone's feet, either.

>> I'm not positive if the current pkg-plist is correct, either.  I
>> generated this one with a find -newer on my machine.  I believe it
>> to be more correct ... and ... of course it includes the new hacks'
>> files.

Doug> 	Well, instead of casting aspersions and including a huge patch
Doug> in your PR you should have taken the time to look at the current
Doug> plist and realize that it's sorted, as the handbook
Doug> recommends. :)

mmm... thought about that about 20 minutes after I sent the pr.  Note
that I did couch my language with "I'm not sure that" .... :).

The number of gle-hacks is growing.  The future potential is cool,
too.

Dave.
